# tests/fixtures/mono-stems

Three synthetic mono WAVs (24-bit / 48 kHz / 0.5 s) generated by ffmpeg's `sine` source.

| File | Frequency | Classification |
|---|---|---|
| `kick.wav` | 220 Hz | drums |
| `snare.wav` | 440 Hz | drums |
| `vox_lead.wav` | 880 Hz | vocal |

Used by Phase 1 P0-1 (pan-law) and P0-2 (24-bit FLAC honesty) validation.

Expected behavior:
- Two automatic groups: `acapella` (vocals only) and `instrumental` (drums).
- Each mono stem upmixed to stereo via `pan` filter.
- With `output.pan_law = -3.0` (default after P0-1), each mono stem contributes
  ~0.7079× per channel — the instrumental group should sum ~3 dB quieter
  than at the legacy 0 dB pan law.
- 24-bit FLAC output should report `bits_per_raw_sample=24` (P0-2).
